# Best Cap Table Software 2026: Carta vs Pulley vs AngelList Stack

## Summary
Comprehensive 2026 market comparison of the three dominant cap table platforms. Carta holds over 80% market share and dominates at Series A+. Pulley has ~15% share and is gaining on Carta, especially at seed to Series B. AngelList Stack has under 5% market share and critically stopped accepting new customers in August 2026, making it effectively unavailable for new startups. All three support SAFEs, priced rounds, option pools, 409A valuations, and ASC 718 reporting. Key differentiator: AngelList Stack's sunset is a critical data point for the stinger's platform-selection guide.

## Key quotations / statistics
- "Carta dominates with over 80% market share, followed by Pulley at around 15%, while AngelList Stack captures less than 5%."
- "AngelList Stack stopped accepting new customers in August 2026."
- "Carta: Free under $1M raised; Build tier ~$2,988/year"
- "Pulley: Free up to 25 stakeholders; $1,200/year Startup tier"
- Carta strengths: "Most comprehensive feature set, largest investor network, strongest 409A valuations, best secondary/tender offer support, deep integration with law firms"
- Carta weaknesses: "Premium pricing, complex interface, brand damage from 2024 data leak controversy"
- Pulley strengths: "Cleaner interface, faster 409A turnaround (3-5 days), competitive pricing, strong customer support"

## Annotations for stinger-forge
- This is the primary source for `guides/01-platform-selection.md` comparison table. AngelList Stack sunset (August 2026) must be flagged prominently as it eliminates one of the three major options.
- The 2024 Carta data leak controversy is a trust signal worth surfacing in the platform-selection guide for founders who store sensitive equity data there.
- Pulley's faster 409A turnaround (3-5 days vs Carta's 5-15 days) is a meaningful differentiator for time-sensitive option grants.
- Contradicts no other sources in research/; consistent with VC Beast, Waveup, and StackFYI.
